pr.title: Decomp campaign: 16 real-C wins + fleet audit tooling

summary: Gates 16 byte-exact functions (5 Dolphin OS wins, 9 register-dump-to-readable colosseum_battle.c rewrites, 2 menu_bag.c asm-wrapper-to-typed-C conversions), starts pointer-arithmetic cleanup in DVDFs.c, and adds a real-C debt audit + richer fleet dashboard so the campaign can track asm-wrapper/register-var/raw-pointer debt repeatably.
decomp_lessons: Functions can be byte-exact matched while still being 'register-dump' garbage (raw r0-r31 locals mirroring register allocation); converting those to real parameter/variable names and control flow is a distinct readability pass that must re-verify the objdiff match, not just a cosmetic rename.

OSInterrupt.c's __OSMaskInterrupts/__OSUnmaskInterrupts needed `#pragma peephole off` plus renaming the internal call target from __OSSetInterruptMask to SetInterruptMask (matching the real symbol) to keep byte-exact output after cleanup.

menu_bag.c asm-wrapper functions (fn_8004EADC, fn_80050844) were converted from `asm void fn(...) { #include ".../fn.s" }` stubs to typed C using a new BagCacheEntry struct, proving asm wrappers in this codebase are often just unconverted matches, not unmatched functions.

DVDFs.c FST (file system table) access was still raw `FstStart_8047A7CC[entrynum*3+N]` pointer arithmetic; introduced a DVDFstEntry struct + DVD_FST_* macros as typed accessors without changing codegen or regressing existing matches.
smart_moves: Added tools/decomp_work/audit_real_c_debt.py: a repeatable audit (regex-based) for asm wrappers/stubs, tracked .inc includes, raw pointer-offset lines, and register-named (rN) locals across all tracked src/*.c, emitting build/real_c_debt_audit.json + .md for campaign planning.

Wired the debt audit and a permuter_status.json feed into tools/decomp_work/fleet_dashboard.py (band_win_stats, debt_stats, permuter_status) so the live dashboard shows real-C debt trend alongside the ASM-bucket/permuter telemetry, not just raw match percentage.

Restored the dashboard's ASM bucket and added per-target Windows permuter telemetry (cores, workers, effective slots, budget, queue/done/wins) to make fleet lane health visible during multi-agent campaigns.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: `#pragma peephole off` combined with renaming an extern call target to the compiler-emitted symbol name (SetInterruptMask vs __OSSetInterruptMask) is a specific lever for keeping matches byte-exact through readability cleanup of low-level OS functions.

For register-dump functions, replacing manual r3/r4/.../r31 = ... sequences with direct nested calls (e.g. fn_80239EE8(0xEC64, ctx, fn_80205B8C(param1), 0, 0, param2, 0, 0x18A)) preserved the byte-exact match while removing the fake register variables entirely.
naming_conventions: New struct BagCacheEntry (5x u32 words) for the menu_bag.c item cache replaces raw `u8 lbl_803A6AB0[]` byte-array typing.
New DVDFstEntry struct + DVD_FST_TYPE_MASK/DVD_FST_ENTRY/DVD_FST_WORD/DVD_FST_TYPE_NAME/DVD_FST_PARENT_OR_START/DVD_FST_NEXT_OR_LENGTH macros standardize DVD filesystem table field access.

key_files.role: 9 functions (fn_80242FEC, fn_80243C5C, fn_802457B0, fn_80247048, fn_802471B0, fn_80248170, fn_8024E0CC, fn_8024E320, fn_802516C4) rewritten from raw register-dump C to readable parameter/variable-based C, preserving 100% objdiff matches.
fn_8004EADC and fn_80050844 converted from asm-wrapper stubs (#include .s) to typed C using new BagCacheEntry struct; first real decompilation of these two functions.
__OSMaskInterrupts/__OSUnmaskInterrupts: added #pragma peephole off and renamed call target to SetInterruptMask to preserve byte match while cleaning up.
Started pointer-arithmetic cleanup: introduced DVDFstEntry struct and DVD_FST_* accessor macros for the FST table without regressing existing matches (readability-only, no new match claimed).
New tool: regex-based repeatable audit of asm wrappers, .inc includes, raw pointer-offset lines, and register-named locals across src/, for campaign debt tracking.
Extended with debt_stats(), permuter_status(), band_win_stats() to surface real-C debt and Windows permuter fleet health in the live dashboard.
